title: South Carolina

jokes.text: Can you fuck off?
jokes.setup: Polite formal exchange about appointments and meetings
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutally direct dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selina's inability to maintain political niceties even in formal settings. Economy of language is superb.

jokes.text: Well, how come you failed me in algebra, Mr. Hennick?
jokes.setup: Someone claiming they've always tried to be there for the character
jokes.punchline: Bringing up a specific academic failure from presumably decades ago
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity of remembering and holding onto academic grievances creates unexpected humor.

jokes.text: How would you like the chance to help lead a visibly repugnant underdog to historic victory?
jokes.setup: Typical campaign recruitment pitch
jokes.punchline: Honestly describing the candidate as 'visibly repugnant'
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty disguised as a sales pitch. The phrase 'visibly repugnant' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Which one of you donkey-dongs was in charge of the balloons?
jokes.setup: Victory celebration moment
jokes.punchline: Selina's ridiculous insult 'donkey-dongs'
jokes.explanation: Selina's inability to deliver a normal insult. 'Donkey-dongs' is absurd enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: It's universal. Men hate women, women hate themselves. The Geraldine Ferraro principle.
jokes.setup: Discussion of 'man up' slogan resonating
jokes.punchline: Cynical explanation of gendered political messaging
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark political insight delivered with clinical precision. The reference to Ferraro adds historical weight.

jokes.text: Full bald eagle.
jokes.setup: Selina asking for a waxing appointment and saying she's feeling patriotic
jokes.punchline: Requesting full pubic hair removal styled as patriotic imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language connecting patriotic imagery to intimate grooming. Absurd but character-specific.

jokes.text: that I was the blackest white woman he ever met.
jokes.setup: Selina recounting what civil rights leader Dr. Jordan Thomas said to her
jokes.punchline: The awkward racial comment she thinks is a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Selina's tone-deafness about race. Sets up later payoff.

jokes.text: She's not even all-black.
jokes.setup: Ben pointing out she's running against an actual black person
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive and problematic response
jokes.explanation: Escalates the racial tone-deafness. Character-perfect but uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Plus, I'm gonna be Lion-Kinging Little Richard all around till I tear my rotator cuff.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining her campaign strategy
jokes.punchline: Turning Disney movie into verb while referencing her baby
jokes.explanation: Creative verb construction and absurd campaign imagery. Original but slightly forced.

jokes.text: I thought everybody kind of did the same thing.
jokes.setup: Gary asking for more responsibility and Catherine defending his role
jokes.punchline: Catherine's complete misunderstanding of campaign hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine naivety about how organizations work. Believable character moment.

jokes.text: No talking! / No talking! / I'm hearing moving!
jokes.setup: Selina trying to sleep on the plane
jokes.punchline: Escalating demands for silence, ending with complaints about movement
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Selina's impossible demands. Gary's echoing adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: OK, I'm gonna go hang myself from a sturdy pipe, and I'm not even gonna bother jacking off.
jokes.setup: Jonah's disappointment with sixth place finish
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific suicide plan with TMI sexual detail
jokes.explanation: Very Jonah - inappropriate oversharing combined with dramatic self-pity. Specific details make it memorable.

jokes.text: Don't math me, Lloyd.
jokes.setup: Lloyd correcting Jonah's percentage calculation
jokes.punchline: Jonah turning 'math' into a verb to avoid being corrected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant verb creation showing Jonah's anti-intellectual stance. Perfectly economical.

jokes.text: Math is a plot invented by the Chinese to make smart Americans feel dumb.
jokes.setup: Jonah rejecting mathematical correction
jokes.punchline: Conspiracy theory about mathematics itself
jokes.explanation: Absurd conspiracy theory that's perfectly in character. Sets up math subplot beautifully.

jokes.text: You were invented by Islamic scholars in the 7th century.
jokes.setup: Lloyd correcting that Islamic scholars invented modern math
jokes.punchline: Jonah applying the same logic personally
jokes.explanation: Perfect childish comeback that accidentally makes no sense. Great structural repetition.

jokes.text: Oh! Saint Theresa's gray-haired gunt, they're fucking again?!
jokes.setup: Discovering his mother and stepfather are engaged again
jokes.punchline: Elaborate religious-anatomical exclamation
jokes.explanation: Creative profanity construction, though relies somewhat on shock value. Very Jonah.

jokes.text: I'll jam my fist up my dick hole and pull out a 40-piece set of Danish cutlery when you're president!
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming he'll be president
jokes.punchline: Impossibly graphic and specific imagery to express disbelief
jokes.explanation: Incredibly specific absurd imagery. The Danish cutlery detail makes it memorably surreal.

jokes.text: not a human pool skimmer last used to de-spunk a Provincetown hot tub party!
jokes.setup: Comparing Selina to Jonah as candidates
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor combining pool maintenance with gay resort town reference
jokes.explanation: Creative insult construction with specific geographic reference. Somewhat shock-value dependent.

jokes.text: Spoiler alert: It's Danish cutlery.
jokes.setup: Offering to send wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Casual reference back to his impossible anatomical feat
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure with perfect economy. The casual delivery makes the grotesque reference funnier.

jokes.text: You can't just replace Gary with another lesbian and think that I'm not gonna notice
jokes.setup: Marjorie bringing Selina tea instead of Gary
jokes.punchline: Selina's assumption that all efficient assistants are lesbians
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina logic - stereotyping while accidentally being right. Character-specific observation.

jokes.text: I learned from an Afghani warlord. / We should put him on the payroll. / You killed him in a drone strike. / Oh, well.
jokes.setup: Marjorie explaining her tea-making skills
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation and dismissal of assassination
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with dark political satire. Selina's casual 'oh well' is perfectly callous.

jokes.text: You've been taking fashion advice from a man who dresses like an overgrown ventriloquist dummy.
jokes.setup: Discussing Gary's fashion sense
jokes.punchline: Specific and vivid description of Gary's appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific visual metaphor that perfectly captures Gary's aesthetic. Very quotable.

jokes.text: These are fresh bagels from New York, which I'm very excited about 'cause the president hates bagels--shh-shh-shh-- and she can't even be in the same room with donuts.
jokes.setup: Blake trying to be a good host
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about Selina's food neuroses
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Blake's nervous oversharing, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: When are you and I gonna have that pussy-eating contest?
jokes.setup: Jonah greeting Amy
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual suggestion
jokes.explanation: Very Jonah but relies heavily on shock value rather than cleverness. Character-appropriate but weak craft.

jokes.text: What about Department of the Exterior?
jokes.setup: Jonah demanding vice president
jokes.punchline: Amy's nonsensical cabinet position
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay on Interior Department. Simple but effective misdirection.

jokes.text: It would be funny if you were American.
jokes.setup: Mike's 'McLintalk' wordplay
jokes.punchline: Wendy's dismissive response questioning his nationality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Mike's attempt at cleverness. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Remembering things and reporting them back to people is not my strong suit, but I could write it down. / Please don't write it down.
jokes.setup: Mike offered to deliver a message
jokes.punchline: Mike's honest admission of incompetence followed by rejection of his solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - self-aware incompetence. The Chinese official's response adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Can I tell you something? My dad said that to a lot of white women. That was his move. Now you know.
jokes.setup: Selina repeating her story about being the 'blackest white woman'
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it was just a pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that completely deflates Selina's prized story. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: I mean, did you at least watch Martin? 'Damn, Gina!' We should go.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to connect with Black senator through pop culture
jokes.punchline: Awkward TV reference met with embarrassed exit
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jonah's tone-deaf attempt at cultural connection. Amy's immediate damage control adds to the embarrassment.

jokes.text: because my mind's on my money... and my money's on my mind. Laid back.
jokes.setup: Jonah asked what's on his mind
jokes.punchline: Quoting Snoop Dogg lyrics inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah cultural appropriation. Character-perfect but relies on recognition rather than creativity.

jokes.text: It's like the fat girl of Cabinet posts.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking for HUD position
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for undesirable cabinet position
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah insensitivity but the metaphor is apt. Economy is good but relies on crude humor.

jokes.text: What the tragic mulatto fuck?
jokes.setup: Selina seeing attack ads
jokes.punchline: Combining racial reference with profanity
jokes.explanation: Creative profanity construction but somewhat problematic. Character-appropriate shock value.

jokes.text: And I'm so black, people are gonna start calling me articulate.
jokes.setup: Selina comparing herself to Kemi in attack ads
jokes.punchline: Reference to condescending praise often given to black politicians
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about racial dynamics. Shows awareness while being character-appropriately tone-deaf.

jokes.text: That's like a dog exploding space shuttle. / Dog chainsaw? / A dog leaf blower? / A dog snowmobile.
jokes.setup: Discussing political dog whistles
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd dog-related metaphors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that completely abandons the original metaphor. Creative absurdity.

jokes.text: Something that makes a NASCAR race look like a Jay-Z concert, you know? / Actually, ma'am, Jay-Z concerts are almost all white people. / Okay, well, right? I know.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting white audiences
jokes.punchline: Kent's correction and Selina's face-saving agreement
jokes.explanation: Great observation about cultural assumptions. Selina's recovery attempt makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Where are you registered? Child Protective Services?
jokes.setup: Mike announcing another adoption
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Mike's parenting is so bad CPS would be involved
jokes.explanation: Quick, dark response that perfectly deflates Mike's happy news. Great economy.

jokes.text: Where are you registered? Child Protective Services?
jokes.setup: Mike announcing another Chinese adoption
jokes.punchline: Gary's suggestion they register with agency that removes children
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant misdirection from baby shower registries to child welfare concerns. Perfectly economical and cutting.

jokes.text: I love that they make clothes in your size now, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike mentioning Gymboree baby store
jokes.punchline: Selina's comment about Mike fitting in children's clothing
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina casual cruelty about Mike's appearance. Economical but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: Liu sent you a message inside Mike. / A misfortune cookie.
jokes.setup: Discussing Chinese message delivered through Mike
jokes.punchline: Fortune cookie wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay that fits the situation. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: And they will not sell you out in a Beijing minute.
jokes.setup: Ben warning about trusting the Chinese
jokes.punchline: Geographic twist on 'New York minute'
jokes.explanation: Simple geographical pun that works in context. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Consider her green-jeaned, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Selina ordering staff member fired for security
jokes.punchline: Ben's pun on 'greenlighted' with clothing reference
jokes.explanation: Mild pun connecting firing someone to their attire. Shows Ben's wordplay tendency but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: He saw a male Jesus.
jokes.setup: Catherine complaining about church visit
jokes.punchline: Treating traditional Jesus depiction as problematic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine over-protectiveness about gender representation. Absurd but character-specific.

jokes.text: When my uncle stole me, I don't remember where he took me, but I do have this recurring dream where I almost find out.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Catherine 'stealing' baby and taking him to church
jokes.punchline: Richard's casual reference to childhood abduction with dream detail
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Richard moment - casual revelation of trauma treated as minor anecdote. The dream detail makes it both specific and haunting.

jokes.text: So was Hitler.
jokes.setup: Marjorie saying she was following orders
jokes.punchline: Invoking Godwin's Law
jokes.explanation: Classic Hitler comparison is predictable but Gary's delivery makes it work. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I've never been more proud I taught that man to ejaculate into a cup.
jokes.setup: Watching Richard's heroic news coverage
jokes.punchline: Catherine's pride in Richard's sperm donation connected to his heroism
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Catherine moment connecting heroism to fertility assistance. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh. Keep talkin' like Reagan, I'll work it like Nancy.
jokes.setup: Tom James discussing 'peace through strength'
jokes.punchline: Sexual reference to Reagan-era politics
jokes.explanation: Clever political/sexual wordplay that's perfectly Selina. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Whoops. I guess we got ourselves a 'Gift of the Magi' situation here.
jokes.setup: Both candidates wanting the same cabinet position
jokes.punchline: Literary reference to ironic situation
jokes.explanation: Apt literary reference showing unexpected sophistication. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Cloud berries are organic and Icelandic.
jokes.setup: Selina demanding fruit, Dan rushing to provide options
jokes.punchline: Dan's pretentious description of exotic fruit choice
jokes.explanation: Shows Dan's attempt to impress through food knowledge. Character-appropriate but not particularly funny or memorable.

jokes.text: He just fucked me right in the ass! / Son of a bitch wouldn't endorse you. / That, too!
jokes.setup: Tom James not endorsing Selina
jokes.punchline: Selina's metaphor misunderstood, then clarified
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with Selina's clarification. Good structure and character work.

jokes.text: I mean, that's like blowing a rape whistle while you're raping somebody.
jokes.setup: Discussing blowing dog whistles in black church
jokes.punchline: Dark analogy about hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Dark but apt analogy for the political hypocrisy. Effective but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: I mean, that's like blowing a rape whistle while you're raping somebody.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting to blow dog whistle in Black church
jokes.punchline: Dan's disturbing but apt metaphor for the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Darkly effective metaphor for political contradiction. Disturbing but accurately captures the hypocrisy of the strategy.

jokes.text: Honeydew? If I wanna pretend to be in the CNN green room, I'll draw a face on Ben's ass and call that Christiane Amanpour.
jokes.setup: Someone offering Selina honeydew
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult combining fruit hatred with CNN reference
jokes.explanation: Incredibly specific and creative insult. The Christiane Amanpour reference is inspired.

jokes.text: I need potheads in my prisons. Otherwise it's just murderers and rapists. It makes for a very unpleasant atmosphere.
jokes.setup: Tanz explaining his prison business needs
jokes.punchline: Treating violent criminals as atmospheric problem solved by drug users
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark corporate logic. The 'unpleasant atmosphere' is perfect understatement.

jokes.text: Permission to roll your breasts, ma'am? / Oh, granted.
jokes.setup: Formal permission request
jokes.punchline: Military formality applied to eye-rolling gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect formal/informal contrast. The phrasing 'roll your breasts' is inspired.

jokes.text: But soon to be the 'Black House.'
jokes.setup: Discussing separation of church and state
jokes.punchline: Racial wordplay on White House
jokes.explanation: Predictable wordplay but appropriate for character and setting.

jokes.text: But the real victims are the police... 'ing.' Policing... that America does in the South China Sea.
jokes.setup: Selina appearing to support police in controversial shooting
jokes.punchline: Last-second pivot to foreign policy
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection that completely subverts expectations. The pause and recovery is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: You know who else thinks that I don't have the intelligence or the 'tentrament' to be president?
jokes.setup: Jonah addressing supporters
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'temperament' while complaining about intelligence questions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - undermining his own argument through incompetence. Character integration is flawless.

jokes.text: Algebra? More like Al Jazeera.
jokes.setup: Jonah's anti-math rant
jokes.punchline: Connecting algebra to Al Jazeera through soundalike wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the math conspiracy. Perfect soundalike wordplay that's completely absurd.

jokes.text: Like my prostate. Mostly black and much larger than we'd like.
jokes.setup: Asked about voter turnout
jokes.punchline: Comparing turnout to medical condition
jokes.explanation: Dark, personal analogy that's perfectly inappropriate. Great economy and unexpected comparison.

jokes.text: I think the Chinese just delivered.
jokes.setup: Power outages affecting black voters
jokes.punchline: Implying Chinese interference to help Selina
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about election interference. Understated delivery makes it more effective.

jokes.text: So go get it. [Long pause as Selina stares at Gary]
jokes.setup: Gary says the remote is on the table
jokes.punchline: Selina's expectation that Gary will retrieve it despite her telling him to get it
jokes.explanation: Perfect power dynamic moment. The silence is the joke - Selina's entitled expectation.

jokes.text: Like Jodie Foster in John Hinckley's diary.
jokes.setup: Saying Gary's name will be all over faith outreach
jokes.punchline: Comparison to obsessive assassination attempt
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference that's unexpectedly specific. Good economy and memorable comparison.

jokes.text: If I need another Washington douche, I'll go to the M Street Rite Aid.
jokes.setup: Firing Dan
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'douche' meaning both jerk and hygiene product
jokes.explanation: Clean douche wordplay with specific geographic reference. Solid construction.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah! This is like looking in a mirror! / Yeah, a hot mirror.
jokes.setup: Jonah meeting attractive Secret Service agent
jokes.punchline: Jonah's attempt to flirt while comparing appearances
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah inappropriate flirting. Character-perfect but relies on his established creepiness.

tmdb_overview: Selina and team head to South Carolina, where she seeks an endorsement from a key leader. Gary gets a long-awaited promotion, while Marjorie excels in her new role.
insight: Political chaos hits 64 jokes in 39 minutes—Veep's densest non-finale episode yet.
